_comment: Standards this exercise app COMMITS to conforming to (Layer 1), plus candidate standards it exhibits that have no WE standard yet (Layer 2). The benchmark validates each declared standard: resolves in the registry? available (active)? used per its real contract (evidence)? A declared standard not yet active is a GAP — the WE work this app drives. `evidence` is a regex proving the app consumes the standard's contract, not a reimplementation. See docs/agent/exercise-app-workflow.md.

standards.concept: selecting a loan row to inspect (now via the master-detail coordinator)
client-side navigation between app views (module bar, detail deep-links)
the loan application's guarded, role-scoped status machine
semantic status chips for state + finding
the loan's immutable history; lifecycle transitions auto-log + a timeline
the proof-of-compliance trace as a normalized DecisionRecord
the pipeline row → trace detail coordination
event-driven notifications to the relevant actor (state change, condition added, doc rejected)

candidateStandards.exhibitedBy: Phase S9 disclosures e-sign (#386) — typed borrower signature + timestamp + audit record
candidateStandards.note: No WE e-signature standard yet; the app hand-rolls a typed-signature form. A reusable surface (typed/drawn signature, timestamp, signer identity, immutable record) — proactive /new-standard feed.

candidateStandards.exhibitedBy: Phase S9 TRID 3-business-day clock (#386) — addBusinessDays / businessDaysRemaining in domain/disclosures.ts
candidateStandards.note: No WE deadline/SLA-clock standard; business-day math + due/overdue evaluation is app-side. Candidate for a reusable deadline-clock standard.

candidateStandards.exhibitedBy: Phase S9 generateInitialDisclosures (#386) — package envelope + Loan Estimate
candidateStandards.note: No WE document-generation standard; the package is assembled app-side. Candidate Layer-2 surface.
